tips: Always verify paddle seating and compatibility before troubleshooting sensor errors

tips: Always use a clean, dry flow tube to ensure accurate ultrasonic measurements

Perform routine calibration checks using a known-good syringe if applicable
Ensure device firmware and PC software are up to date for connectivity stability
Keep battery contacts and charging ports clean to prevent power issues
warnings: Moisture or contamination in the flow path can significantly impact measurement accuracy
Do not perform troubleshooting while a patient test is in progress to avoid data loss

commonIssues: Water circulation or pump failure leading to poor temperature control
Blanket or hose connection not detected by the unit
Over-temperature or under-temperature alarm conditions
Display or control panel malfunction
Low fluid level or air in system causing flow issues
tips: Always verify proper hose and blanket connections before suspecting internal faults
Maintain correct fluid levels using approved solutions to prevent pump damage
Check for kinks or obstructions in hoses during flow-related issues
Allow adequate warm-up time before determining heating performance issues
Perform regular cleaning and preventive maintenance to avoid sensor and flow problems
warnings: Do not operate the unit without proper fluid levels as it can damage the pump
Avoid using incompatible blankets or accessories which may affect performance or safety
Always remove from patient use before troubleshooting temperature or flow issues

Lubricate approved mechanical components per manufacturer guidelines
warnings: Do not use stretcher if latch or locking mechanism is unreliable

Battery or charging issues on powered mobility or transport equipment

Mechanical binding or actuator wear in lifts and beds
